content:
# Trace Element Geochemistry

**Summary**: The study of elements present in small concentrations in rocks.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] analyzed trace elements like Uranium and Vanadium to assess ore quality.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] used trace element chemistry to characterize the basement minerals and sedimentary components.

## Scientific Context

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] Used to identify economic potential and mineral purity.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] used trace element chemistry to characterize the basement minerals and sedimentary components.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] Gamma-ray spectroscopy and PIXE.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Uranium content reached up to 300 ppm, which is significant for industrial use. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 26]])

- Quantified various oxides and elements in granite, gabbro, and schist samples from the local basement. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 235]])
